name: Nostalgia
description: Warm memories from your youth to cherish and enjoy.
technical_summary: Returns era-specific nostalgic facts and memories from configurable fact database based on user era_of_youth preference.
fallback: Do you remember the wonderful music and dances from your youth?

1930s: Do you remember listening to radio programs like The Lone Ranger with your family?
The movies were magical back then! Do you remember going to the picture show?
Shirley Temple was the biggest star. Did you ever tap dance like her?
Families would gather around the radio for evening entertainment.
1940s: Do you remember when a loaf of bread cost just 8 cents?
The Andrews Sisters were singing everywhere. Do you remember their songs?
Victory gardens were in every backyard. Did your family grow vegetables?
Do you remember saving tin cans and rubber for the war effort?
The jitterbug and swing dancing were all the rage!
1950s: On this day in 1955, the first Mickey Mouse Club episode aired!
Do you remember when everyone got their first television set?
Poodle skirts and sock hops were the height of fashion!
Elvis Presley changed music forever. Do you remember hearing him for the first time?
Drive-in movies were the perfect date night. Did you have a favorite?
1960s: Do you remember the first time you heard The Beatles on the radio?
The space race was exciting! Do you remember watching the moon landing?
Woodstock brought people together through music. What a time!
Bell-bottoms and peace signs were everywhere.
Gordon Lightfoot and folk music filled the airwaves.
1970s: Do you remember when the first Star Wars movie captivated everyone in 1977?
Disco fever had everyone dancing! Did you do the Hustle?
Pet rocks were somehow a thing. Did anyone you know have one?
The Bee Gees were on every radio station.
Fonzie and Happy Days brought back the feeling of the 1950s.
1980s: MTV launched and changed how we experienced music. Do you remember?
The first personal computers started appearing in homes.
E.T. had everyone saying phone home. Did you see it in theaters?
Pac-Man fever swept the nation. Did you play video games?
